text: In 1876, I founded the Gold Dollar, one of the first Black weekly newspapers in the South. I founded the Gold Dollar to educate and strengthen the Black community. We were still living in the shadow of enslavement and this made reading, writing, and communication crucial keys to freedom. The Gold Dollar was the first Black-owned newspaper in this capital city, known by some as the first Black newspaper west of the Mississippi. Between 1870 and 1900, this was among 48 Black-owned commercial newspapers in Texas. Nowadays, the Gold Dollar building is not just a former newspaper office, though. This is the last standing structure of Wheatville, a freedom colony in Central Austin.
